mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series expand, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflationary pressure below the 0.2% monthly threshold reduces real interest rate expectations and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative in >55% of months following disinflationary readings, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive quarterly returns in >50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing the 2017 baseline index level confirms normalized manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in >60% of weeks when the index crosses the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.
